{"id":"CP-q4un","title":"MQTT-only subdir watcher create propagation is flaky","description":"Summary: The new MQTT-only regression `test_mqtt_only_watcher_create_in_subdir_propagates_to_server_and_peer` is non-deterministic. In one run it timed out waiting for schema entry `created-via-watcher.txt` (schema remained empty), but immediate reruns passed. This indicates a race in subdir watcher/create propagation rather than a deterministic logic failure.

Files to investigate:
- tests/sync_integration_tests.rs (test_mqtt_only_watcher_create_in_subdir_propagates_to_server_and_peer and polling helper timing)
- src/sync/dir_sync.rs (handle_subdir_new_files scheduling/order)
- src/sync/transport/subscriptions/subdir_task.rs (subdir schema apply/refresh ordering)
- src/bin/sync.rs (watcher create event path to CRDT schema update)

Implementation steps:
1. Reproduce with repeated runs of the single create test and paired mqtt_only_watcher_ suite.
2. Capture logs/ordering around watcher create, schema publish, and subdir schema receive/apply when timeout occurs.
3. Fix ordering/race so subdir schema entry appears deterministically under MQTT-only mode.
4. Keep regression test and add stability guard (or explicit retry semantics) to prevent sporadic timeouts.

Example:
Before: occasional timeout waiting for `created-via-watcher.txt` in subdir schema despite no code changes between runs.
After: repeated runs consistently converge and schema entry appears every time.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jes5199@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-19T04:21:54Z","created_by":"Jes Wolfe!","updated_at":"2026-02-19T04:21:54Z","work_type":"mutex"}

old_value: {"id":"CP-k870","title":"Honor ignore_patterns in load_workspace_existing_file_node_ids","description":"load_workspace_existing_file_node_ids says it applies the same scan filters as scan_dir_recursive, but it only checks hidden names and extensions; it never applies options.ignore_patterns. In subdirectory scans that use ignore globs (e.g. '*.txt'), node IDs from ignored peer files in other directories are still counted as 'existing', so load_linked_entries can incorrectly preserve missing linked entries that should be filtered out. Fix: apply should_ignore(filename, \u0026options.ignore_patterns) check in the loop alongside the existing hidden/extension checks. File: crates/commonplace-schema/src/directory.rs:224-228. Identified by Codex review of PR #117 at commit 8418be6e.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jes5199@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-17T20:25:01Z","created_by":"Jes Wolfe!","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:25:01Z","work_type":"mutex"}

new_value: Added should_ignore(filename, &options.ignore_patterns) check in load_workspace_existing_file_node_ids alongside existing hidden/extension checks. 3-line fix in directory.rs:230-232.
{"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"CP-vogw","title":"Cache workspace UUID traversal across subdirectory scans","description":"load_workspace_existing_file_node_ids rebuilds a full workspace UUID map on every scan_directory call by invoking build_uuid_map_from_local_schemas from the workspace root. create_documents_for_null_entries recursively calls scan_directory for each subdirectory (src/sync/dir_sync.rs:2428-2435). In workspaces with many node-backed directories this turns sync into repeated full-tree schema walks (effectively quadratic behavior), which can slow or time out initial syncs. Fix: compute the UUID set once per sync pass (or pass a cache through the call stack). Identified by Codex review of PR #117 (CP-ohck fix). File: crates/commonplace-schema/src/directory.rs:215","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jes5199@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-17T20:20:47Z","created_by":"Jes Wolfe!","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:20:47Z","work_type":"mutex"}

old_value:
{"id":"CP-xmws","title":"Add repo/branch metaphor to workspace directory structure","description":"Currently all files start from the root workspace directory with no organizational hierarchy beyond subdirectories. Propose adding a convention where:

- The toplevel synced directory represents 'repos'
- Each toplevel subdirectory is a 'branch'
- Branches can contain a `__branch.json` metadata file describing:
  - Which branch it was forked from (parent branch name/id)
  - Fork point (commit hash or timestamp)
  - Branch name/description
  - Any other branch metadata

This restructures the workspace from a flat file tree into a repo-like model where users can have multiple named branches of content that share history through fork relationships.

Example structure:
```
workspace/
  main/
    __branch.json    # { "name": "main" }
    file1.txt
    subdir/
  feature-x/
    __branch.json    # { "name": "feature-x", "forked_from": "main", "fork_point": "..." }
    file1.txt        # diverged copy
    new-file.txt
```

This is a prerequisite for adding git-like checkout/branching CLI commands to commonplace-sync. The branch metadata enables:
- `commonplace-branch create <name>` — fork current branch
- `commonplace-branch list` — show all branches with fork relationships
- `commonplace-checkout <branch>` — switch active branch
- Potential future merge operations using CRDT history

Design questions to resolve:
1. Should branches share document UUIDs (same CRDT doc, diverged state) or get new UUIDs (full copy)?
2. How does this interact with the existing node-backed subdirectory model?
3. Should the sync client be branch-aware (only sync one branch at a time) or sync everything?
4. What happens to existing flat workspaces — migration path?","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jes5199@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:05:31Z","created_by":"Jes Wolfe!","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:05:31Z"}

old_value: {"id":"CP-bhul","title":"CLI: Switch from HTTP to magenta/MQTT commands","description":"The CLI should NOT use HTTP internally. HTTP is for external clients only. The CLI is a magenta client — all internal communication goes through MQTT: magenta commands for imperatives, blue document edits for configuration changes, document reads for queries. This ensures uniform auth (biscuits), logging (blackboard), and observability through the reactive graph. Requires refactoring commonplace-status, commonplace-branch to use MQTT instead of reqwest HTTP calls.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jes5199@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:38:23Z","created_by":"Jes Wolfe!","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:38:23Z"}

Remaining CLI tools (show, log, replay, link) still use HTTP — they need server-side MQTT handlers for commit history and SSE equivalents before they can be converted.

new_value: {"description":"When run_directory_mode exits for re-root: (1) spawned subdir MQTT tasks are fire-and-forget tokio::spawn and not tracked — they survive into the next iteration. (2) MQTT subscriptions from the old root's sync tasks remain active on the broker after abort(), causing old-branch traffic to enter the shared broadcast channel. Fix: either track JoinHandles and unsubscribe on teardown, or recreate the MQTT client between roots.","priority":2}
old_value: {"id":"CP-kjoh","title":"Abort orphan subdir sync tasks on re-root","description":"When run_directory_mode exits for re-root, spawned subdir MQTT tasks (from spawn_subdir_watchers) are fire-and-forget tokio::spawn and not tracked. They survive into the next run_directory_mode iteration. Need to collect JoinHandles and abort them in the teardown section. Low severity: tasks reference old root topics and stale file_states, so they'll fail harmlessly, but could cause brief cross-branch writes.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jes5199@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T08:31:37Z","created_by":"Jes Wolfe!","updated_at":"2026-03-06T08:31:37Z"}

new_value: Implemented checkout via sync agent re-root. Sync agent listens on MQTT control topic, exits with ReRoot, caller restarts with new root UUID. Server-authoritative startup on re-root with force_overwrite_local. Validated through 10 iterations of codex review.

old_value: {"id":"CP-g9ea","title":"Establish repo/branch directory convention","description":"Migrate from flat fork-based branching to nested /repo/branch/ layout. Currently branch create forks the workspace root as an independent tree. The 'Reachability Is Liveness' principle requires branches to be linked from the root tree to be syncable. New layout: /myapp/main/ is the main branch, /myapp/feature-x/ is a feature branch. Each branch dir contains __branch.json with ForkManifest. {"description":"Root cause identified: After decode failure, cyan sync recovers the schema CRDT, but file re-materialization is skipped because handle_subdir_new_files is gated behind http_recovery_disabled (root_task.rs:241), which is always true in the sync binary MQTT runtime (sync.rs:2409 calls set_sync_http_disabled(true)). The recovery path needs an MQTT-based file content fetch (cyan for files, not just schemas) or needs to use the existing CRDT state to re-write files without an HTTP round-trip. Tests: test_root_decode_failure_recovers_missing_file_from_snapshot and test_subdir_decode_failure_recovers_missing_file_from_snapshot."}
old_value: {"id":"CP-4kp8","title":"CI: decode failure recovery tests flaky on GitHub Actions","description":"test_root_decode_failure_recovers_missing_file_from_snapshot and test_subdir_decode_failure_recovers_missing_file_from_snapshot consistently fail on CI. Both time out waiting for file recovery content — 'Last content: \u003cmissing\u003e'. Sometimes one passes while the other fails, sometimes both fail. Passes locally. Likely a timing issue — CI runners are slower so the 20s timeout isn't enough, or the recovery path has a race condition that's exposed under load.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jes5199@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T22:38:02Z","created_by":"Jes Wolfe!","updated_at":"2026-03-06T22:38:02Z"}

new_value: CLI tools (branch, checkout) now query the server directly via HTTP instead of reading local .commonplace.json files. This means they work immediately after init without waiting for the sync agent to materialize directories.
Always include sync field explicitly in CRDT partial update — CRDT merge cannot delete keys by omission
Show full root_id instead of truncating to 8 chars
{"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"CP-dk9l","title":"CRDT-based file materialization after schema recovery","description":"After cyan snapshot recovers a schema (decode failure or resync), the sync agent needs to re-materialize missing files. Currently this is gated behind http_recovery_disabled (root_task.rs:438, subdir_task.rs:358) and skipped. Instead: walk the recovered schema entries, check which files are missing on disk, and for each missing file with a node_id, use the existing CRDT state (file_states + Yjs docs already in memory from MQTT subscriptions) to write the content. If CRDT state isn't available for a file, request it via cyan sync for that specific doc. This fixes CP-4kp8.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jes5199@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T22:43:06Z","created_by":"Jes Wolfe!","updated_at":"2026-03-06T22:43:06Z"}

new_value: Removed overly conservative http_recovery_disabled gates in root_task.rs that blocked handle_subdir_new_files entirely. The function already handles mqtt_only mode internally. Fix extracts schema from CRDT state for bootstrap, and passes decoded MQTT schema for loop updates.
Fixed by CP-dk9l — decode recovery tests now pass consistently (1.27s). The root cause was http_recovery_disabled gates blocking file materialization after cyan schema recovery.
Implemented spawn_subdir_watchers_from_schema to extract node-backed dirs from CRDT state instead of HTTP. Root task tracks latest decoded schema for reactive discovery. Commit c72de0a.
Fixed: use node_id from schema instead of identifier (file path) for UUID parsing. When use_paths=true, identifier is a path string that always fails Uuid::parse_str(). Commit fee4d5e.
Removed handle_schema_change_with_dedup HTTP path and related dead code (http_recovery_disabled, last_schema_cid, updated_cid). -70 lines. Commit f007987.
